title: Quarter Kevu is keeping VCARB's title machine rolling
meta: Published: 17 March 2026 | F1CC News Feature Desk
caption: Lead image: Quarter Kevu in the VCARB garage.
body:
Quarter Kevu has spent the opening phase of 2026 defending the standard VCARB set over the last two seasons. The recent history behind the garage matters here: VCARB produced the 2024 drivers' champion in Lukus Wright, then repeated the feat with Kevu in 2025. In Australia he was quick enough to fight at the front of the midfield. In China he turned that speed into a podium, finishing second behind only Eetu Väisänen at Shanghai.

That result matters because it was not built on chaos alone. Kevu qualified fourth in China, then kept himself in the right window through the race to capitalise when the Ferrari and Red Bull runners began to shuffle. He now sits on 27 points after three rounds, which keeps him exactly where a reigning champion should be: in the title conversation and rarely far from the front.

More importantly, VCARB are no longer a team merely trying to prove they belong at the front. Kevu has looked composed in traffic, sharp on restarts, and unusually effective at turning decent grid positions into proper points. The team are still the reference point in their own garage, and the rest of the field is still trying to catch them.

There is still a long season ahead, and midfield form tends to swing quickly once upgrades start to land. But if Kevu keeps pairing strong qualifying with clean race execution, VCARB may spend far less time as a background team and far more time as the one everyone else is trying to catch.
